item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This gives me hope, no matter what the IRC members say.

Obligatory note, IRC reviewers don't give negative critique because they want to crush hopes, they do so if they feel that a concept or draft would not be well-received if posted to the mainsite. This sentence kiiiiiinda reads a little accusatory regarding reviewer intent. Not a huge issue (I'm giving you the benefit of the doubt here), just maybe something to be more aware of, especially since reviewers are all unpaid volunteers helping out in their own free time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This, THIS is why I'm still writing at my original idea for a first skip. Because first skips still have the ability to be great, like this one.

This gives me hope, no matter what the IRC members say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So nothing really to say regarding content that hasn’t been said before. Just wanted to point out that “heart palpitations” are essentially just knowledge of an arrhythmia so saying staff experienced both is redundant.

item.title: great work
item.content:encoded: i can tell how much time and thought was put into this work. the beginning chunk of the document almost turned me away from reading it as it didn't seem to have a strong 'hook', but as it progressed i became much more intrigued. it's a massive article, but builds on previous lore in an interesting way while offering new paths to continue in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is how I read this. Kind of like "oops, I done fucked up… here, lemme jus' fix that right quick."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
first skip? that's certainly not what i was expecting. this is some really superlative, detailed, painstaking world- and narrative-building. I love it, even if it conflicts with some of my headcanon (poor nine tailed fox… also, GO TEAM KATE!

now, its 4 in the morning and I may just be tired, but what was the "deific entity" that fought against 3391 and rebooted reality? was it Kate? did I fail to read between the lines?

I'd like to eventually go through this to do a little grammar/punctuation cleanup, just to make things flow a bit more smoothly. but overall— wow. really fantastic work.

I wonder if you've written any skips since this one… I'd love to read them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …did Ion just punch reality in the face to undo the apocalypse caused by some neo-sarkic going off the reservation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How the hell does this, six months later, have not even 50 points? This is criminal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, time to delete my first SCP! My little 12 person cult in the woods can't hold a candle to a literal Archon resting under the Mediterranean Sea, nor your ambitious execution and continuous exploration of the concept of a "build-your-own-chaos-god kit".

Jokes aside, this is genuinely amazing and the author should be proud. This was their baby and it shows. There are a few typos early on in the archived SCP files from the alt timeline, but they don't take away from the impact of this much, if at all. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are too much stuff that isn't of my liking, and generally it was maybe too ambitious a project. But it was well written, and it is astounding as a first project. I think I'll keep it a no-vote for now, and maybe I review my opinion next time I come across this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to finally see this on the mainsite! I’m happy to see how this has evolved into a skip I personally consider great. Amazing work, and I’m looking forwards to your future stuff.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of all the twists and turns this took this being your first article blew me away the most. This was legitimately so good and high-stakes that by the time I hit the supplement I thought I was reading a doom-con entry I'd yet missed.

The only thing I can think of right now that needs work is some log content; namely, I agree with most of what's being said here regarding it being hard to follow. Other than that, though? I'm actually hesitant to keep reading more articles, because this was the most satisfying one I've read all day.

Not sure if this may be a mistake though...
This...

<E11-2 stops broadcasting entirely. All anomalous humanoids collapse, and cease lifesigns. E11-2 and -7 reach Squad Leader’s position.>

…and this…

<The remains of Epsilon-11 traverse the facility. They encounter several anomalous organisms, all of which are only nominally aggressive, fleeing after only minimal fire. Several fail to acknowledge the team altogether. E11-1, -2 and -7 stop outside of the Site 104 security control room. Epsilon-11 is reunited at this point.>

seem to be in error? I'm pretty sure -7 was dead by this point, and it's supposed to be -9.

At any rate, +1, with gusto!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love me some long articles. And this one was brilliant! That it's your first skip, even more so. It really shows how much time you've spent here reading, and I love all the threads you've tied together and the way you've expanded Sarkic lore.

I particularly love that Anatolian false narrative, as it shows (one of?) the fork(s) in the road where Neo-Sarkicism began to split off from mainline faith. False or not, you just created a great piece of (fictional) history with that, as it's probably the first piece of lore highlighting that.

+1! Also, I'm adding this to the hub, because it certainly deserves to be there.

P.S: Thanks for crosslinking to SCP-2815! :) It's my first (and hitherto only) skip as well. That said, yours is better by far than what mine was when I published it. Kudos again!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well! First Scip. Hopefully I don't crash and burn.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=e-ERBaMmu-E

Well, paint me red and call me 612, this is an amazing first skip! +1s all around!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well! First Scip. Hopefully I don't crash and burn.

Pfft.

Don't mind me, I'm just as impressed as everyone else is.

Carry on and have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good lord. I want to link this to every person thinking about writing a first SCP, but none of them would ever post if this was the standard.

There is a lot of stuff going on here, almost too much for my taste. You have an underwater flesh mass AND Sarkic mythology AND an MTF action piece (incidentally, I agree that was particularly hard to follow) AND an end-of-the world scenario AND more Sarkicism AND a reality-bending piece. At some points it became unwieldy and threatened to become cliched, but it manages to hold together, which is impressive in itself. Easy +1.

I'm not sure about 'Project Hubris', though, it's a bit too cutesy/melodramatic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First skip?

This is your first skip?

Well hot damn, am I looking forward to your second.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"First skip".

Holy shit.

After all the classic trappings — weird stuff underwater, flesh where it doesn't belong, lengthy exploration logs (that second one is especially good!) — "first skip" was not what I was expecting to see at the end of this. Holy crap, you've done a remarkably good job here! Now for the nitpicks! :D

It feels like it’s shaking to Command, are we reading seismic disturbances?

I think something went wrong here. And now I wish I had been recording typos, because there were more than a few and this thing is so long, there's no way I'd remember any of them by this point.

The other thing I wanted to comment on was that second exploration log, with the MTF. The ending was gripping, but the middle is rather confusing. It seemed like the team split up at one point — when -1 and someone else (?) are facing down the copied skip — but afterward, they get back together with the rest of the team, and I could never keep track of when they were apart and when they were together. Not sure if it's really important.

All I know is this needs more votes, because damn. :O

item.title: SCP-3391
item.content:encoded:
Well! First Scip. Hopefully I don't crash and burn. I've been lurking for several years now and one day the phrase "False Archon" just popped into my head, followed by the phrases "Artificial Demiurge" and "Juvenile God" respectively.

This is the child of those thoughts.

Review Credits:

Thanks to Not a Seagull, Dr. Dromeus, Lucky Man John and an anonymous friend for all the feedback. You were all indispensable.

Image Attribution:

Martensia spp, Batemans Marine Park, near Pretty Beach NSW by "RoyEdward"
Expl1567 - Flickr - NOAA Photo Library by "Submarine ROF 2006, NOAA Vents Program."
